title: The Mice And The Weasel
narrative_elements: weasel disguises herself with flour
naive mice are caught one by one
old mouse recognizes the trap
old mouse challenges the disguise with wit

title: The Murderer And The Mulberry Tree
narrative_elements: murderer kills someone on the road
murderer lies about bloody hands blaming mulberry tree
pursuers capture murderer
murderer crucified on the mulberry tree
tree declares justice served

title: The Two Mules
narrative_elements: wealthy mule parades proudly with bell
barley mule walks quietly
robbers ambush both mules
wealthy mule wounded and robbed
barley mule escapes unharmed

title: The Musician At Home
narrative_elements: musician plays in echoing room
echo makes him think he is excellent
musician performs on stage
audience throws stones and drives him off

title: The Swallow And The Nightingale
narrative_elements: swallow invites nightingale to live with her
nightingale weeps and refuses
nightingale explains grief of ancestors

title: The North Wind And The Sun
narrative_elements: wind and sun argue who can make man disrobe
north wind blows fiercely but man wraps tighter
sun shines warmly and man removes cloak voluntarily

title: The Old Woman And The Wine Jar
narrative_elements: old woman finds empty wine jar
jar still carries pleasant smell of wine
woman inhales deeply and praises what once was

title: The Man And The Old Woman
narrative_elements: young man carries exhausted old woman out of pity
aroused by lust he acts on impulse
disguises act with euphemism about carving flesh
old woman asks to repeat the act later

title: The Ostrich
narrative_elements: war breaks out between beasts and birds
ostrich is captured
ostrich shows birds her head to seem like a bird
ostrich shows beasts her feet to seem like a beast

title: The Panther And The People
narrative_elements: panther falls into pit
some attack her, others bring bread
panther escapes overnight
panther returns to wreak havoc
panther spares those who showed mercy

title: The Partridge And The Roosters
narrative_elements: man buys partridge and puts it among roosters
roosters peck and chase partridge away
partridge grieves thinking it is due to being foreign
partridge sees roosters fighting each other
partridge accepts the situation philosophically

title: The Widow And Her Lover
narrative_elements: widow mourns faithfully at husband's tomb
soldier guarding nearby crucifixes meets widow
soldier seduces widow over repeated visits
guarded corpse stolen from cross
widow offers husband's corpse to save soldier from punishment

title: The Pine Tree And The Wedges
narrative_elements: woodcutters split a pine tree with wedges
pine tree reflects on the source of its pain
pine tree blames not the axe but its own offspring wedges

title: The Swan And His Owner
narrative_elements: man buys swan believing swans sing beautifully
swan stays silent at dinner party
swan sings only when facing death
owner regrets not having butchered the swan earlier

title: The Wolf And The Shepherd
narrative_elements: wolf follows flock harmlessly for a long time
shepherd gradually trusts wolf as guardian
shepherd leaves wolf in charge of sheep
wolf slaughters most of the flock
shepherd blames himself for misplaced trust

title: The Woman In Labour
narrative_elements: woman labours on the ground
husband urges her to use the bed
woman refuses citing ironic logic about the place of conception

title: The Wolf, The Raven And The Ram
narrative_elements: wolf sees raven sitting on a ram without reproach
wolf envies raven's immunity from criticism
wolf reflects that his same actions would draw hostile response

title: The Raven And The Snake
narrative_elements: raven spots sleeping snake in the sun
raven pounces hoping for an easy meal
snake bites raven
dying raven laments his fatal greed

title: The Raven, The Stork And His Beak
narrative_elements: stork injures wife's eye with his beak
stork flees out of shame
raven questions whether stork carries same beak
raven points out running away solves nothing

title: The Raven, The Swallow And The Seasons
narrative_elements: swallow and raven argue about who is more beautiful
raven claims swallow's beauty only lasts in spring
raven boasts of enduring beauty through all seasons

title: The Thief And The Lamp
narrative_elements: thief lights lamp from Jupiter's altar
thief robs temple by his own holy light
divine Religion speaks and condemns the thief
Religion forbids lighting lamps from sacred fire

title: The Rich Man And The Tanner
narrative_elements: tanner moves in next to rich man
rich man objects to foul smell
tanner predicts rich man will adapt
rich man insists he will not lose his sense of smell

title: The Rich Man And The Tanner
narrative_elements: rich man lives next to tanner and hates the smell
rich man repeatedly urges tanner to move
tanner always postpones
rich man eventually habituates and stops complaining

title: The Rivers And The Sea
narrative_elements: rivers bring fresh water to the sea
rivers complain that sea turns their water salty
sea replies that rivers should simply not come if they object

title: The Two Soldiers And The Robber
narrative_elements: two soldiers encounter a robber
one soldier flees while the other fights bravely
robber is repelled by the brave soldier
cowardly soldier returns boasting after danger has passed
brave soldier rebukes the coward's false bravado

title: The Sailors And The Stones
narrative_elements: passenger threatens sailors in bad weather
passenger says he will pelt them with stones
sailor wryly wishes they were somewhere stones could be found

title: Jupiter and Modesty
narrative_elements: Jupiter forgets to include modesty when creating man
Jupiter releases modesty into the world
modesty accepts condition that carnal love not be present with her
modesty flees wherever carnal love appears

title: The Sheep, The Shepherd And His Cloak
narrative_elements: shepherd climbs oak tree to shake down acorns for sheep
sheep eat acorns and unknowingly eat shepherd's cloak
shepherd discovers ruined cloak
shepherd rebukes sheep for destroying the clothing they provide wool for

title: The Shepherd And The Wolf Cub
narrative_elements: shepherd finds wolf cub and raises it
shepherd teaches wolf to steal from neighbours
grown wolf warns shepherd that he may lose his own sheep

title: The Snake And The Wasp
narrative_elements: wasp lands on snake and stings him repeatedly
snake cannot escape its tormentor
snake positions himself under approaching wagon wheel
snake chooses death to take wasp down with him

title: Zeus And The Snake
narrative_elements: zeus holds wedding and all animals bring gifts
snake brings rose in his mouth
zeus accepts gifts from all animals
zeus refuses gift from snake's mouth due to distrust of snake

title: The Soothsayer And The Theft
narrative_elements: soothsayer claims to predict the future
soothsayer's house is robbed
soothsayer rushes home in distress
bystander ironically points out soothsayer's failure to foresee own fate

title: The Stag And His Reflection
narrative_elements: stag admires his horns but disparages his legs in reflection
hunters chase the stag
stag outruns hunters thanks to his legs
stag's horns tangle in branches and he is captured
stag laments that what he prized destroyed him, what he despised could have saved him

title: Zeus And The Tortoise
narrative_elements: zeus invites all animals to wedding feast
tortoise alone does not attend
zeus questions tortoise's absence
tortoise says there is no place like home
zeus punishes tortoise by making her carry her home everywhere

title: The Man And The Weasel
narrative_elements: man captures weasel
weasel pleads she clears house of mice
man sees through weasel's self-serving argument
man points out weasel hunts mice for herself not for him
man kills the weasel

title: The Witch On Trial
narrative_elements: witch claims to avert divine wrath and earns money doing so
witch is accused of sacrilege and condemned
bystander ironically asks why she could not ward off mortal plans if she can deflect divine anger

title: The Monk And The Abbots
narrative_elements: monks pray for each successively worse abbot to die
each dead abbot is replaced by a worse one
wise monk prays for the worst abbot to live long
wise monk explains that each replacement has been worse

title: The Ants And The Pigs
narrative_elements: ants gather large store of grain for winter
pigs arrive and scatter and eat all the grain

title: The Dog In The Manger
narrative_elements: dog lies in manger full of hay
cattle approach wanting to eat
dog blocks them baring teeth
cattle point out dog begrudges them what is useless to him

title: The Goat And His Reflection
narrative_elements: wolf chases goat up a cliff and besieges him
both go away hungry and thirsty
goat drinks and sees his reflection boasting of bravery
wolf eavesdrops and attacks the boasting goat
goat begs forgiveness blaming the water for his words

title: The Lion And The Horse
narrative_elements: lion spots horse grazing and approaches as a doctor
horse suspects trick but plays along
horse pretends to have splinter in hoof
horse kicks lion in the face and escapes
lion laments approaching horse kindly rather than as an enemy

title: The Wolf And The Nurse
narrative_elements: nurse threatens to throw crying child to the wolf
wolf waits nearby hoping the threat is real
child falls asleep and nurse's threat is not carried out
wolf goes away empty-handed having trusted idle words

title: The Dogs And Their Commander
narrative_elements: dogs at war with wolves elect an Achaean commander
commander delays and explains the diverse composition of dog army
wolves are uniform and unified
commander argues unity of enemy vs diversity of allies makes battle risky

title: The Merchant, The Donkey And The Salt
narrative_elements: donkey accidentally falls in stream and salt dissolves lightening load
donkey repeats trick deliberately on next trip
merchant realizes and loads donkey with sponges
sponges absorb water and burden doubles

title: Aesop And His Ugly Mistress
narrative_elements: mistress adorns herself but finds no suitors
aesop offers blunt advice about her looks
mistress orders aesop beaten for honesty
bracelet is stolen and mistress questions slaves
aesop refuses to be intimidated because he was punished for telling the truth

title: Aesop And The Runaway Slave
narrative_elements: slave describes cruel master and plans to flee
slave claims he has done nothing to deserve punishment
aesop points out that guilt now makes his situation worse
slave abandons escape plan

title: Momus And The Gods
narrative_elements: gods compete to create finest things
zeus makes man, athena makes a house, poseidon makes a bull
momus judges and finds fault with all three creations
momus criticises bull's eyes, man's hidden heart, house's immobility

title: The Statue Of Hermes And The Treasure
narrative_elements: poor man prays daily to idol for wealth
man grows poorer despite prayers
man smashes idol in frustration
gold hidden inside idol scatters about the room
man exclaims god yields to force what he refused to prayer

title: The Camel In The River
narrative_elements: camel crosses swift river
camel relieves herself in the water
camel sees excrement float ahead of her
camel laments inversion of natural order

title: The Bride And The Two Grooms
narrative_elements: two men compete for same bride, rich man wins
poor man retreats heartbroken to country home
storm diverts wedding procession
bride's hired donkey runs to familiar nearby shelter (poor man's home)
storm and divine fortune unite bride with true lover

title: Aphrodite And The Slave-Woman
narrative_elements: slave woman bedecked with gifts from master provokes wife
slave woman credits Aphrodite for her good fortune
slave worships Aphrodite daily
Aphrodite appears in dream to deny any role in slave's perceived beauty

title: Pompey And His Soldier
narrative_elements: soldier presents as effeminate but robs Pompey's baggage
soldier takes oath denying theft with lewd gesture
enemy challenges Romans to single combat
effeminate soldier volunteers and beheads the enemy
Pompey praises soldier but identifies him as the thief using his own gesture

title: The Young Man And The Swallow
narrative_elements: young man has lost everything gambling
out-of-season swallow chirps in winter
man gambles away his last cloak thinking spring has come
snowstorm hits and man is left naked
man finds swallow dead in the cold and blames it for deceiving him

title: The Bull And The Bullock
narrative_elements: young bullock mocks old working bull
old bull pulls plow silently and makes no reply
time comes for sacrifice and young bullock is chosen not the old bull
old bull tells bullock the reason for his idleness was that he was being fattened for sacrifice

title: The Bear And The Fox
narrative_elements: bear boasts of never harming a human corpse
bear claims great regard for mankind
fox notes bear's respect would be more impressive if it extended to the living

title: The Beauty Contest Of The Animals
narrative_elements: zeus holds beauty contest for animal babies
monkey brings ugly baby claiming it is most beautiful
gods laugh at the ugly monkey baby
monkey insists her offspring is most beautiful to her eyes

title: The Dog And His Bell
narrative_elements: dog bites people and master ties bell on him as warning
dog parades about shaking bell proudly
old dog corrects young dog saying bell marks shame not honour

title: The Bulls And The Wagon
narrative_elements: bulls strain to pull wagon into town
wagon keeps creaking loudly
driver rebukes wagon for making noise when those carrying it are silent

title: The Oxen And The Butcher
narrative_elements: oxen decide to destroy all butchers
oxen sharpen horns for battle
elderly ox argues butchers kill humanely and alternatives would be worse
ox warns they will die twice over in unskilled hands

title: The Rooster And The Cats
narrative_elements: rooster hires cats as litter bearers and parades proudly
fox warns rooster the cats look hungry not dutiful
cats grow hungry and tear the rooster apart

title: The Wolf, The Dog And The Collar
narrative_elements: wolf meets fat and well-fed dog
wolf asks how dog gets so much food
dog reveals a man feeds him
wolf notices bare spot on dog's neck from iron collar
wolf rejects luxury of captivity in favour of freedom

title: The Dolphins And The Whales
narrative_elements: dolphins and whales wage fierce war
sprat emerges and offers to mediate
dolphins scornfully reject small creature's offer to arbitrate their noble conflict

title: The Wolf And The Lame Donkey
narrative_elements: donkey goes lame from thorn and notices wolf
donkey pretends to ask wolf to remove the thorn as a favour before dying
wolf removes thorn from donkey's hoof
freed donkey kicks wolf in the face and escapes
wolf laments taking up healing when he should have stayed a butcher

title: The Jackdaw And The Eagle
narrative_elements: eagle seizes lamb from flock for its chicks
jackdaw tries to imitate eagle and swoops on lamb
jackdaw's claws tangle in wool and he is caught
jackdaw laments trying to imitate eagles

title: The Farmer And The Sea
narrative_elements: farmer watches ship sink and blames the sea
sea takes form of a woman to speak in her defence
sea argues winds not she are the cause of storms
sea invites farmer to sail on a calm windless day

title: The Farmer And The Birds
narrative_elements: birds steal seeds from farmer's field
birds learn to flee when farmer asks for sling
farmer devises trick using codeword bread to mean sling
birds fooled and stoned while not expecting weapon
birds warn cranes that humans say one thing and do another

title: The Old Man And His Sons
narrative_elements: dying old man asks sons to bring bundle of rods
sons cannot break rods together
sons easily break rods one by one
old man teaches that unity provides strength division brings vulnerability

title: The Lizard And The Snake
narrative_elements: snake grabs lizard from behind
snake opens mouth to swallow lizard
lizard grabs twig and jams it sideways in snake's mouth
lizard escapes while snake is foiled

title: The Monkey And The Fox
narrative_elements: monkey asks fox to share part of her tail to cover monkey's bare behind
fox refuses cruelly saying she would sooner drag tail through filth than share any with monkey

title: The Horse And His Groom
narrative_elements: groom sells horse's barley to innkeepers
groom spends evenings drinking
groom spends days grooming horse's appearance
horse tells groom real beauty requires proper nourishment not just external care

title: Mercury And The Two Women
narrative_elements: mercury visits two women who host him poorly
mercury offers to grant each woman a wish
mother wishes for her son to have a beard quickly
prostitute wishes to attract anything she touches
mother's baby grows a beard and screams
prostitute laughs at the baby but her nose adheres to her touching hand

title: The Mouse In The Pot
narrative_elements: mouse falls into lidless pot of broth
mouse drowns in broth while eating greedily
mouse accepts death philosophically having feasted well

title: The Sow And The Wolf
narrative_elements: pregnant sow labours on the ground
wolf approaches offering to serve as midwife
sow recognises the wolf's deceptive intent
sow refuses wolf and bids him only to stand away

title: The Young Man And The Prostitute
narrative_elements: prostitute deceives and hurts young man repeatedly
prostitute flatters young man claiming he is her favourite
young man knows she is lying but says the words please him anyway

title: The Ram And The Wolf
narrative_elements: wolf walks by wall
ram on top of wall makes rude remarks
wolf gnashes teeth but acknowledges situation gives ram his bravado

title: Socrates And The Slave
narrative_elements: slave rudely addresses Socrates
Socrates knows slave has seduced master's wife
Socrates warns slave that pleasing who he ought not to will not go unpunished
Socrates notes slave fails to please those who matter

title: The Sick Man And His Wife
narrative_elements: sick man vows hundred oxen to gods for recovery
wife asks where oxen will come from
sick man says he counts on never leaving his sickbed so gods cannot collect

title: The Oak Tree And The Reed
narrative_elements: oak tree boasts of strength against wind
oak tree mocks reed for yielding to every breeze
fierce wind blows
oak is uprooted while flexible reed survives

title: The Sheep, The Goat And The Sow
narrative_elements: man loads sheep goat and sow onto donkey for transport to city
goat and sheep are quiet knowing they are wanted for milk and wool
sow screams loudly fearing she alone faces death

title: The Lion And The Farmer's Daughter
narrative_elements: lion falls in love and asks farmer for daughter's hand
farmer says daughter fears lion's teeth and claws
lion agrees to have teeth and claws removed
defenceless lion is beaten and driven away by farmer

title: The Stork And The Cranes
narrative_elements: cranes steal seed from farmer's field
stork lives among cranes but does no harm
farmer sets snare and catches stork along with cranes
innocent stork is held accountable for birds he associated with

title: The Goatherd And The Goat
narrative_elements: goatherd throws rock at straying goat breaking her horn
goatherd begs goat not to tell the master
goat points out the broken horn itself will tell the story

title: The Crab And His Mother
narrative_elements: mother crab tells son to walk straight
son asks mother to lead the way
mother cannot walk straight herself
son accuses mother of being out of her mind

title: The Kite And His Mother
narrative_elements: kite falls deathly ill
kite begs mother to offer vows at shrines for his recovery
mother fears shrines are polluted by kite's past sacrilege
mother says she cannot pray for one who desecrated holy places

title: The Deer And His Mother
narrative_elements: fawn notes mother is bigger swifter and has horns for defense
fawn asks why mother fears hounds
mother admits that despite her advantages the bark of a dog makes her faint and flee

title: The Farmers, The Donkey And The Lion Skin
narrative_elements: donkey dresses in lion skin to appear fierce
donkey terrorises farmers' crops
wind blows off lion skin revealing donkey
farmers recognise donkey and beat him to death

title: The Wolf And The Donkey
narrative_elements: wolf visits sick donkey
wolf touches donkey asking where pain is
donkey says wherever wolf touches hurts

title: The Fox And The Lion Hunting
narrative_elements: fox and lion hunt as partners each playing their role
fox grows jealous of lion's larger share of prey
fox hunts alone without lion's protection
fox is captured and killed by hunters

title: The Snake, The Eagle And The Farmer
narrative_elements: snake and eagle fight
farmer frees eagle from snake's grip
snake poisons farmer's drinking water in revenge
eagle swoops down and knocks cup from farmer's hands saving him
